rules.name: 60-30-10 Rule
rules.description: Use a dominant color (60%), secondary (30%), and accent (10%).
rules.application: Background as dominant, surfaces as secondary, CTAs as accent.
rules.example: White background (60%), light gray cards (30%), blue buttons (10%).
rules.anti_pattern: Equal distribution of 5 bright colors throughout the UI.

rules.name: Semantic Colors
rules.description: Assign consistent meaning to colors: success, warning, error, info.
rules.application: Green for success, amber for warning, red for error, blue for info.
rules.example: Green checkmarks for completed items, red badges for errors.
rules.anti_pattern: Using red for both errors and primary actions, creating confusion.

rules.name: Color Accessibility
rules.description: Never use color alone to convey information.
rules.application: Pair color indicators with icons, text, or patterns.
rules.example: Error states using red color plus an error icon and descriptive text.
rules.anti_pattern: A form where invalid fields only change border color with no text feedback.

rules.name: Neutral Palette
rules.description: Build a comprehensive neutral scale for text, borders, and backgrounds.
rules.application: Create 8-10 shades from near-white to near-black.
rules.anti_pattern: Using pure #000000 and #ffffff only, with no intermediate shades.

rules.name: Color Temperature
rules.description: Keep color temperature consistent within a palette.
rules.application: Warm-tinted neutrals with warm accents, or cool with cool.
rules.example: Warm grays (#f5f0eb) paired with warm accent colors (#e86833).
rules.anti_pattern: Mixing cool blue-grays with warm orange-browns randomly.

rules.name: Contrast Ratios
rules.description: Meet WCAG AA minimum contrast ratios: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text.
rules.application: Verify all text-background combinations with a contrast checker.
rules.anti_pattern: #999999 text on #ffffff background (2.8:1 ratio, fails AA).

rules.name: Dark Mode Considerations
rules.description: Invert the luminance hierarchy, not just the colors, for dark themes.
rules.application: Use elevated surfaces instead of shadows; reduce saturation for comfort.
rules.anti_pattern: Simply inverting all colors, resulting in eye-straining neon on dark backgrounds.
